advisory_findings.finding: Lead-in shift from optional-tone ('ask Claude to choose') to action-lead (rule 2, check_prompts.md) is semantically correct and reads peer-flat (Bootstrap voice). No register slippage detected.
advisory_findings.evidence: Line 71: 'If the volume is in the way, ask Claude to choose the fixes that aim for optimal function in the next session.' — directive verb ('ask') + semantic action summary.
advisory_findings.fix_hint: No action required. Lead-in meets rule check_prompts.md § 2 (action lead-in, under 20 words).

check_writing.md_1_banned_words.result: No instances of banned words (honest, delve, landscape-as-verb, importantly, crucial, substrate, leverage-as-verb, synergize, paradigm shift). Practice (noun) not present in body.

check_writing.md_2_ritual_ban.result: No ritual/practice-as-noun/ceremony language. Word 'exercise' used correctly (line 5: 'the previous exercise'). No ceremony framing.

check_writing.md_3_stale_markers.result: No stale markers, session dates, 'fixed' retrospectives, historical notes in body. Maintainer block below line 92 carries dates (2026-04-29) and context; body is clean.

check_writing.md_4_register.result: Register is peer-flat (Boris + Rory + Sutherland). Lead-ins read directive without coach-register. Subagent explanations are technical-precise. Body reads confident, not apologetic. Tone matches Bootstrap voice (Godin warmth + Sutherland reframe + Siilasmaa action).

check_writing.md_6_credit_original.result: Frameworks credited in maintainer notes (Rumelt, Roger Martin, Rory Sutherland). Body prose does not name the frameworks by author — that's a maintainer/annotation detail. Body stays focused on the work, not the attribution pedigree.

check_writing.md_8_maintainer_vocabulary.result: No smuggling of maintainer terms into body. 'Subagent' appears in body (lines 23, 25) with explanation context on first use (line 23: runtime-differentiated 'Claude Code calls them **subagents**' vs Cowork 'agents'). 'Rumelt's strategy kernel' used in body but earned in context — student is about to apply it, so naming it here is on-time.

check_writing.md_13_value_prop_leak.result: No defensive vendor-framing or positioning-posture creep. Body teaches the move, not the curriculum's positioning. No 'unlike vendor' / 'real practitioners' / 'grounded in actual' framing.

check_writing.md_9_over_hedge.result: Line 71 optional-tone is pedagogically deliberate (learner choice on volume), not reassurance: 'If the volume is in the way...' is a genuine branching condition, not an apology for the move. Appropriate.

check_student_facing.md_1_aspirational.result: Framing is aspirational (line 12: 'Multi-agent's second shape') and forward-moving (line 14: 'the synthesizer's output'). No corrective gap language ('you already think', 'you're missing').

check_student_facing.md_2_earn_technical.result: Key terms earned on first use: (a) 'subagents' (line 23, runtime-differentiated with explanation); (b) 'Rumelt's strategy kernel' (line 9, immediate context 'diagnosis, guiding policy, coherent actions'). Terms are not redefined per-mention; fluency is trusted after first earn.

check_student_facing.md_5_golden_path.result: Body teaches the path. Troubleshooting and optional refinements (lines 71, 75, optional second prompt) are clearly marked optional or delegated to Claude ('ask Claude to choose'), not inserted as branching paths in the golden-path narrative.

check_prompts.md_1_no_placeholders.result: Three fenced blocks (lines 33–45, 53–66, 77–79). No [BRACKETS] placeholders. All three reference scrollback implicitly ('my curated memory/') or name destinations explicitly ('to module-3/stances/'). No broken open-hooks or placeholder slots.

check_prompts.md_2_action_lead_in.result: Lines 29 (first prompt): 'Ask Claude to spawn three subagents with different stances, then apply Rumelt's kernel...' — command verb, semantic summary, under 20 words. Lines 50 (Cowork fork): same shape. Lines 75 (optional): 'You choose fixes. Aim for optimal function in the next session that runs on this.' — directional, under 20 words.

check_prompts.md_3_canonical_shape.result: All three prompts follow canonical shape: lead-in + label + blank line + fenced block. First two prompts are in paired divs (.rt-code / .rt-cowork) with unified lead-in above both. No 'copy >' prefix, no '(end of prompt)' closer. Shape is clean.

check_prompts.md_5_chain_by_backref.result: Prompts chain via explicit memory reference ('my curated memory/', 'previous exercise integrated the retrievals into') and destination paths ('module-3/stances/', './crux.md'). Deterministic paths throughout. No fuzzy fallback.

analysis.compendium_coverage: All three compendiums loaded and applied. No rule gaps.
analysis.fiction_free: Target file is an exercise; no fictional citations or output-generation claims examined (not applicable).
analysis.body_closure_check: Section before Close (lines 82–91) examines closure-prose and debrief-prompt tone. No dangling pronouns or bypass-the-agent reflexes post-trim (rule check_writing.md § 15). The doubt-naming close (lines 85–90) stands on its own and does not reference trimmed content.
analysis.rail_guard_check: No teacher framing in body (check_student_facing.md § 26 hard-grep: 'the trainer shows', 'say it plainly', 'before students start' — none present). No narration of student state post-jargon (check_student_facing.md § 3: 'you've done', 'you're building' — absent; good standing). No room-share commands (hard-grep 'tell the room', 'share with your neighbor', 'call it out' — absent).
analysis.prompt_isolation: Prompts are self-contained (check_prompts.md § 6). First two prompts spawn subagents and synthesize; optional third is a delegation to Claude. No leaking downstream pipeline into the subagent instructions.
analysis.runtime_fork_compliance: Paired divs at lines 27–47 (.rt-code / .rt-cowork). Both forks preserve pedagogical content identically ('each reads my curated memory', 'spawn...in parallel', 'apply Rumelt's kernel'). Runtime difference is terminology only ('subagents' vs 'agents'). Unified lead-in above both forks. Compliant with check_prompts.md § 10.

summary: File passes all writing-class checks. Register is peer-flat and technical-precise. Prompts are well-structured with action lead-ins, no placeholders, and clear runtime-fork labeling. Body teaches the work without leaking maintainer vocabulary or value-prop positioning. The lead-in change on line 73 (from 'Optional: paste this back...' to action directive) improves semantic clarity and aligns with check_prompts.md rule 2. No violations detected.
